keyFindings: Phase 005's 'inconclusive' label is a downstream reframing of a 4/4 FAIL/FAIL-BLOCKED result table; the decision-record trigger's dispatch_failure clause fired but the required native/Claude baseline comparison was never run (iter 1).

The cli-opencode ADR-001 self-invocation guard is scoped to opencode-invoking-opencode dispatch and does not gate native or cli-claude-code dispatch, making a partial Claude-leg comparison runnable now without an external shell (iter 2).

The tool.execute.before OpenCode plugin hook is the actual load-bearing mechanism for KQ5's enforcement plugin (neither prior lineage cited it); its fail-closed-vs-mutate-only capability is unconfirmed and is a named residual (iter 2).

Mode D is CONFIRMED-in-at-least-one-instance, not merely inferred: the Phase 0 'GENERAL AGENT REQUIRED' self-check present across all 8 /deep:* command files is the exact, cited mechanism behind phase 005's research-mode failure (iter 3).

The ai-council 'route-proof naming drift' is a code-traced, deterministic emitter-vs-validator self-contradiction inside deep_ai-council_auto.yaml (lines 117-118 vs 132-136) that would fail for any executor, not a registry-vs-YAML mismatch (iter 4, iter 8).

KQ4's top-ranked, full-agreement recommendation ('orchestrate dispatches @deep and STOP') in its literal Task-dispatch reading violates orchestrate.md's own documented NDP depth cap; a corrected, NDP-safe registry-reuse fix is proposed (iter 5, softened via iter 9 self-check).

KQ9's 'wait' verdict on FIX-5 is independently reached by the non-GPT glm-max lineage and is reinforced by a coverage argument (FIX-5 does not fix Mode D or latency) — not primarily a self-protective GPT artifact; the 'gate cannot fire' residual is less severe than characterized (iter 6).
Four implementation-ready diffs (Mode D self-check rewrite, ai-council validator fix, orchestrate NDP-safe routing rule, sequencing correction) with an internal precedence ordering (iter 7).
Citation accuracy across both prior lineages is high; this round's corrections come from incomplete cross-referencing and framing choices, not fabricated or misattributed evidence (iter 8).
Adversarial self-check downgrades four of this round's own findings' confidence framing without changing the proposed fixes, applying the charter's own standard reflexively (iter 9).
Per-original-KQ confirmed/sharpened/overturned verdict table and two new KQ6 benchmark-design preconditions (iter 10).
ruledOutDirections: Treating 'Phase 005 was inconclusive' as an evidence-neutral summary (iter 1).
Treating a full external-shell 8-run harness as required before any GPT-vs-Claude comparison evidence can be gathered (iter 2).
Treating both prior lineages' plugin-hook citations (chat-transform hooks) as sufficient mechanism evidence for KQ5 (iter 2).
Treating Mode D as still purely hypothetical/unmeasured (iter 3).
Treating glm-max's loop_protocol.md citation as the strongest available Mode D evidence (iter 3).
Treating the ai-council fix as registry-vs-YAML drift rather than an internal emitter/validator contradiction (iter 4).
Treating the ai-council naming fix as independent of KQ1/KQ6 benchmark validity (iter 4).
Treating 'orchestrate dispatches @deep and STOP' as NDP-safe in its literal reading (iter 5).
Treating gpt-fast-high's vaguer 'resolve via deep.md' phrasing as interchangeable with glm-max's explicit dispatch wording (iter 5).
Treating 'wait on FIX-5' as primarily evidence of GPT self-protective bias (iter 6).
Treating the KQ1 gate as entirely unreachable without an external shell (iter 6).
Treating this round's corrections as evidence of prior-lineage citation inaccuracy (iter 8).
Treating the ai-council validator failure as merely probable rather than code-traced-certain (iter 8, upgrade not rule-out).
Treating iteration 5's NDP violation claim as certain against both possible readings of the prior recommendation (iter 9).
